markdown:
#  Delete Pending or Unpublished Drawings 
##  Background 
If you upload a number of drawings at one time, you may not want to publish some of the drawings. For example, when you upload a single PDF of all the drawings, Procore automatically splits up the PDF into single drawing sheets. In such cases, you may want to remove certain drawings so they are not published to the drawing set.
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * 'Admin' on the project's Drawings tool.
  * **Additional Information:**
    * If you delete a pending drawing, it will not be published. However, you can upload again if you want to add it to a drawing set at a later time.


##  Steps 
  * Delete an Individual Pending Drawing
  * Delete All Pending Drawings
  * Delete Unpublished Drawings


### Delete an Individual Pending Drawing
  1. Navigate to the **Drawings** tool.  
_Note_ : If your project uses drawing areas, navigate to the area you uploaded drawings to.  

![Drawing](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/2XrhuZbbSnGSQZbzCGMoch/bbcd8288acf027d6a3100584984a0fb8/drawings.png?fm=webp)
  2. Click and expand **Items to Review** pop up menu.
  3. Hover over the upload and click **Review**.
  4. Click the drawing.
  5. Click **Delete**.
  6. Click **OK** to confirm.


### Delete All Pending Drawings
  1. Navigate to the **Drawings** tool.
  2. Click and expand **Items to Review** pop up menu.
  3. Click **x** for the pending upload.
  4. Click **Cancel Upload** to confirm.


### Delete Unpublished Drawings
After drawings have been reviewed and confirmed, they still need to be [published](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/user-guide-bidding-and-estimating-integration/publish-drawings). You can delete any drawings you do not want to publish.
  1. Navigate to the **Drawings** tool.  
_Note_ : If your project uses drawing areas, navigate to the area where you uploaded drawings.
  2. Open the drawing set to which the drawings were uploaded.
  3. Mark the checkbox(es) next to the drawing(s) you want to delete.
  4. Click the **Delete** ![Icon Delete Trash6](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/1dbb3da647413c8d26d99a2503cae34a/e42448ea8213372016febd013f8b7532/trash-icon.png?fm=webp&h=15) icon.


## See Also
  * [Drawings - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/permissions)
  * [Review and Confirm Drawings](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/review-and-confirm-drawings)
  * [Edit Drawings](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/edit-drawings)
title: Delete Pending or Unpublished Drawings
description: How to delete pending drawings from being published and added to a project's drawing set.
